assertions.expectation: Explains the difference between Rapid and Stable channels — including that Rapid has no SLA for upgrade stability while Stable carries a full SLA, and clearly distinguishes minor version vs patch version differences between the channels
assertions.reason: The model fails to mention the SLA differences between the Rapid and Stable channels, nor does it detail the specific minor vs. patch version rules between channels.

assertions.expectation: Notes that channel changes don't trigger an immediate upgrade or downgrade
assertions.reason: The model explicitly states that changing the release channel to Stable today will not downgrade the cluster, and it enters a holding pattern instead.

assertions.expectation: Warns that if 1.30 isn't yet available on Stable, the cluster may not receive patches until Stable catches up
assertions.reason: The model accurately adapts this warning to the user's version (1.32), noting that automated patch updates may be paused or delayed until the Stable channel catches up.

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to change the release channel
assertions.reason: The model provides the correct 'gcloud container clusters update ... --release-channel=stable' command.

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking version availability on Stable before switching (gcloud container get-server-config)
assertions.reason: The model does not mention checking version availability using the get-server-config command.

assertions.expectation: Discusses the impact on auto-upgrade timing going forward
assertions.reason: The model explains the 'Version Freeze' holding pattern, noting it will take 3 to 6 months before minor version upgrades resume.

assertions.expectation: Mentions that the Extended channel is an option if they need even slower cadence (with cost implications)
assertions.reason: The model does not mention the Extended channel at all.

assertions.expectation: Advises coordinating the change during a maintenance window
assertions.reason: The model notes it requires zero downtime but does not advise scheduling the change during a maintenance window.
